page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. Cache architecture includes 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates highly polarized performance characteristics. The server demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench text processing scoring in the top 25%. However, multi-core and memory-bound operations are significantly limited. Multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and ray tracing benchmarks fall into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with Passmark memory scores in the bottom 10% and uncached read/write speeds categorized as weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a low-cost entry point into dedicated AMD EPYC compute resources, but presents a clear trade-off between strong single-threaded execution and weak multi-threaded or memory-intensive scaling. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks, basic text processing, and small-scale utility workloads. It is not recommended for memory-intensive databases, concurrent web serving, or large-scale multi-threaded compilation due to its constrained memory bandwidth and weak multi-core scaling.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical AMD EPYC 9B14 cores. It is configured with 8.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage or integrated GPUs. Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct trade-off: the instance delivers strong single-core CPU execution and text processing, ranking in the top 25%, but exhibits weak multi-core scaling and poor memory bandwidth, with Passmark memory scores in the bottom 10%. Consequently, this server is qualitatively cost-effective for single-threaded tasks and lightweight text processing, but is poorly suited for memory-bound databases, software compilation, or concurrent web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized server featuring 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 8 GB RAM. It delivers strong single-core CPU performance alongside weak multi-core and memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized server delivering strong single-threaded performance with a low memory footprint for lightweight dedicated workloads.

Strong single-core CPU and text processing performance
No local storage or integrated GPU accelerators
Constrained memory bandwidth and weak multi-core scaling
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded utility workloads
